skill.name: modelarts-skill
skill.description: Manage Huawei Cloud ModelArts AI platform: notebooks, training jobs, inference services, resource pools, VPC networks, and more through a secure SDK wrapper.
skill.summary: Huawei Cloud ModelArts platform integration with modular design, OBS storage support, and environment variable authentication.
skill.author: modelarts-agent

security_audit.summary: This is a legitimate Huawei Cloud ModelArts integration skill. All 1169 static findings were evaluated: the vast majority are false positives in Markdown documentation files showing CLI command examples (577 external_commands hits). Critical findings for Windows SAM database and C2 keywords are false positives matching Huawei Cloud project IDs and unrelated comments. The code has a well-designed security model with credential isolation - API keys are stored in memory and masked before any LLM-facing output. Real concerns are limited to expected cloud SDK behaviors: environment variable access for credentials and network calls to Huawei Cloud APIs.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Access for API Credentials
low_findings.description: The skill reads environment variables for ModelArts access keys (AK/SK) and project configuration. This is expected for a cloud SDK integration but represents a real security consideration. The code properly masks credentials before returning data to the LLM.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Project ID in Code Examples
low_findings.description: A Huawei Cloud project ID (0f2788726a80f4ec2fecc00b5844a0c2) is hardcoded as a default parameter value and in documentation examples. While a project ID is not a secret credential, hardcoding internal identifiers is a data leakage concern.

low_findings.title: Network Requests to External APIs
low_findings.description: The skill makes HTTP requests to Huawei Cloud ModelArts API endpoints. This is expected functionality for a cloud platform integration skill. All requests are to documented Huawei Cloud API endpoints using authenticated sessions.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Commands in Documentation Files
dangerous_patterns.description: Markdown documentation files (references/*.md) contain shell command examples using backtick notation. These are documentation-only and not executed. Confirmed false positive after file content review.

dangerous_patterns.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm References
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner flagged references to KMS (Key Management Service) as weak cryptography. The skill uses Huawei Cloud ModelArts SDK's built-in authentication (auth_by_apig), not custom cryptographic implementations. Confirmed false positive.

dangerous_patterns.title: C2 Keyword Pattern Match on Project IDs
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner flagged Huawei Cloud project IDs as potential C2 beacon identifiers. The string '0f2788726a80f4ec2fecc00b5844a0c2' appearing in code is a legitimate cloud project ID used for API routing, not a command-and-control indicator.

dangerous_patterns.title: Dynamic Import Pattern Matches
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner flagged __import__() expressions in bootstrap files. These are standard Python module loading patterns used for the skill's modular architecture. No dynamic code execution from untrusted input occurs. Confirmed false positive.

dangerous_patterns.title: Windows SAM Database False Positive
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner flagged multiple files as containing Windows SAM database references. File content review confirms no SAM database artifacts exist. The pattern match was triggered by unrelated comments about security and credential handling.

content.user_title: Manage Huawei Cloud ModelArts AI Platform
content.value_statement: Cloud AI teams struggle with managing complex ModelArts resources through web consoles and fragmented scripts. This skill provides a unified command interface to manage notebooks, training jobs, inference services, resource pools, VPC networks, and KMS keys through natural language.
actual_capabilities: Create, start, stop, and delete ModelArts notebook instances with EVS or OBS storage
Create, list, stop, and delete training jobs with configurable resources and images
Manage inference services (v1 and v2 API) including create, deploy, update, and scale operations
Manage dedicated resource pools and node pools with batch node operations (reboot, resize, lock, migrate)
Query VPC networks, subnets, security groups, KMS key pairs, and SWR image repositories
Manage workspaces, authorization settings, quotas, and scheduled events on the ModelArts platform
limitations: Only works with Huawei Cloud ModelArts platform, not other cloud providers
Cannot create or modify cloud infrastructure outside ModelArts (e.g., direct VPC creation, IAM policy management)
Requires pre-configured ModelArts SDK environment or valid AK/SK credentials
Model training jobs run in the cloud and results depend on dataset availability and resource limits

use_cases.title: Data scientists managing AI experiments
use_cases.description: Data scientists can create notebooks, attach OBS storage, run training jobs, and deploy models without switching between web console and CLI tools.

use_cases.title: MLOps engineers automating workflows
use_cases.description: Platform engineers can script resource pool creation, batch node operations, and inference service lifecycle management across multiple workspaces.

use_cases.title: Cloud architects managing ModelArts infrastructure
use_cases.description: Cloud architects can query VPC configurations, manage security groups, audit workspace authorizations, and monitor resource quotas through conversational commands.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Notebook Instance
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new ModelArts notebook instance named 'experiment-1' with 2 vCPU 8GB flavor, using image ID '278e88d1-5b71-4766-8502-b3ba72e824d9', with 5GB EVS storage and auto-stop after 1 hour.

prompt_templates.title: Submit a Training Job
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a training job named 'bert-finetune' using command 'python train.py --epochs=10 --batch_size=32' with image 'dev-custom/openclaw-fxb-test:latest' in the production pool 'pool-abc' with 1x GPU flavor.

prompt_templates.title: Deploy an Inference Service
prompt_templates.prompt: Deploy a real-time inference service in the dedicated pool using model 'bert-base-chinese' with 2 instances of flavor 'modelarts.vm.cpu.8u' and enable auto-scaling.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Platform Resources
prompt_templates.prompt: List all running notebooks, check available training flavors, show dedicated pool capacity, and display current workspace quotas for project '0'.

output_examples.input: Show me all running notebooks and their status
output: Total EVS storage used: 25GB across all instances

output_examples.input: Check the status of my training job bert-finetune

best_practices: Initialize authentication first using the auth module before making any API calls to ensure credentials are properly loaded
Use evs storage for development notebooks and obs storage for production workloads to separate data persistence
List available flavors and images before creating resources to confirm capacity and compatibility
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode project IDs in automation scripts - use environment variables or the auth module to resolve them dynamically
Do not share credential values or unmasked AK/SK output in logs or conversations - the auth module provides masked status for this purpose
Do not create resources without checking existing quotas and capacity limits first to avoid API errors

faq.question: What credentials do I need to use this skill?
faq.answer: In ModelArts Notebook environment, credentials are auto-detected. For local use, you need ModelArts AK, SK, project ID, and region name set as environment variables or provided at initialization.

faq.question: Does this skill expose my API keys to the AI model?
faq.answer: No. The auth_manager stores credentials in memory only and masks them before returning any data to the AI. The LLM only sees masked status information.

faq.question: Which ModelArts API versions are supported?
faq.answer: The skill supports both v1 (legacy) and v2 inference APIs, training jobs API, notebooks API, resource pools API, and management APIs including workspaces and authorization.

faq.question: Can I manage resources across multiple Huawei Cloud regions?
faq.answer: Yes. Each authenticated session targets a specific region. You can initialize separate credentials for different regions and manage resources in each region independently.

faq.question: What storage types are supported for notebooks?
faq.answer: Both EVS (block storage) for development and OBS (object storage) for production workloads are supported. OBS storage requires a DEW secret name for encryption.

faq.question: Does this work with dedicated resource pools?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill supports creating, listing, and managing dedicated resource pools and node pools, including batch operations on nodes and plugin management.
